title: The to-do list parked outside the bedroom door
subtitle: A short bedtime visualisation for physically externalising tomorrow's tasks before sleep.
excerpt: A short visualisation for Day 7 of the SLEEP program. You see tomorrow's list clearly, write it on a mental page, and leave it outside the bedroom door. The tasks are held; you do not have to hold them.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short visualisation for Day 7 of the SLEEP program: moving tomorrow's list out of the body and placing it somewhere it will be held without you. The tasks are not lost. They are parked.
Lie down and close your eyes. Take two slow breaths.
Let the tasks and concerns that are still circling come into view. Don't try to stop them, just watch them arrive.

Imagine a piece of paper in your hands. Watch yourself writing each task onto the page, one by one, until everything that was in your head is on the paper. When you feel you have it all, fold the paper once.
Now see yourself walking to your bedroom door, opening it slightly, and placing the folded paper just outside. Close the door.
Notice the quiet inside the room. The list still exists. It is outside the door, waiting, but it is no longer in the bed with you.
Come back to the bed. Feel your weight in it. The list will be there in the morning. Tonight it is not yours to hold.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
